subject: Databases
title: Third Normal Form (3NF)
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Third Normal Form (3NF).

hook.text: A users table has city and zip_code. But city depends on zip_code, not directly on the user.

buildup.text: 3NF removes transitive dependencies. No non-key column should depend on another non-key column.

discovery.text: Fix: create a zip_codes table mapping zip to city. Users stores only zip_code.

twist.text: In practice, many teams stop at 3NF. Higher normal forms exist but rarely justify the added complexity.

climax.text: 3NF is the sweet spot. It eliminates most redundancy without making the schema unmanageable.

punchline.text: 3NF: no column depends on another non-key column.

quiz.question: What does Third Normal Form (3NF) eliminate?
options: Transitive dependencies where a non-key column depends on another non-key column
All foreign keys from the database
